title: MacOS 15.6.1 Attempting to run mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest causes segmentation fault core dumped
body:
### Issue Description

On MacOS 15.6.1 on MacBook Pro M4 Attempting to run the Microsoft Container Image mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest results in "Segmentation fault      (core dumped)"

I've run the same tests in Docker Desktop and Rancher Desktop and they work and successfully launch SQL Server 2022 container

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.podman --version
podman version 5.6.0
2.podman machine inspect --format {{.Rosetta}}
true
3.podman pull mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
4.podman run --memory=4096M -e "ACCEPT_EULA=Y" -e "MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD=SecurePassword123$" -p 1433:1433 --name sql2022 --rm -i mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
SQL Server 2022 will run as non-root by default.
This container is running as user mssql.
To learn more visit https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2099216.
/opt/mssql/bin/launch_sqlservr.sh: line 28:     9 Segmentation fault      (core dumped) "$@"

### Describe the results you received

WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
SQL Server 2022 will run as non-root by default.
This container is running as user mssql.
To learn more visit https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2099216.
/opt/mssql/bin/launch_sqlservr.sh: line 28:     9 Segmentation fault      (core dumped) "$@"

### Describe the results you expected

SQL Server 2022 will run as non-root by default.
This container is running as user mssql.
To learn more visit https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2099216.
2025-09-15 23:29:06.16 Server      Setup step is copying system data file 'C:\templatedata\master.mdf' to '/var/opt/mssql/data/master.mdf'.
2025-09-15 23:29:06.19 Server      Did not find an existing master data file /var/opt/mssql/data/master.mdf, copying the missing default master and other system database files. If you have moved the database location, but not moved the database files, startup may fail. To repair: shutdown SQL Server, move the master database to configured location, and restart.

.....

comments.body: I'm having the same issue, I've tried both hypervisors: LibKrun and Apple Hypervisor
Same issue, although with the image _mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-CU16-ubuntu-22.04_ on M3 Pro. Works fine with Docker Desktop

Rosetta seems to be disabled but is not shown in the cli or podman desktop. Downgrading podman to 5.5.2 solved the problem
https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

brew tap-new $USER/local-tap
brew tap homebrew/core --force
brew extract --version=5.5.2 podman $USER/local-tap
brew install $USER/local-tap/podman@5.5.2
brew link --overwrite podman@5.5.2
I have the same issue, @m4m4m4 downgrading solution worked for me

Switching from LibKrun (default) to Apple HyperVisor and 
podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"
podman machine stop
podman machine start
fixed the issue for me on Podman v5.7.1.

Qemu-user-static appears to not be working with LibKrun and Apple HyperVisor.

It seams like you can only run one Podman Machine at a time for some reason or I would be running both LibKrun and Apple HyperVisor just for mssql.

Apple containers does work but the container will not show up in the Podman Desktop Apple Container Extension.
container run --os linux --arch amd64 --memory 4g -d -e ACCEPT_EULA="true" mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/rhel/server:2022-latest

@wagneramichael thank you!

Using your commands seems to have fixed my issue. Can you explain why we need to do: `podman machine ssh "sudo touch /etc/containers/enable-rosetta"`?

**EDIT:** Forget about it - here is the source - https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

If you are on Tahoe (macOS 26+) everything is working but Rosetta is **disabled** by default. Follow the blog to enable it.

Can confirm AppleHypervisor and the enable-rosetta trick works on my M4. 

However, this configuration causes an "Illegal Instruction" error when running dotnet build inside a container:  
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/122608#issuecomment-3957753130

Not sure if this is a podman issue or dotnet/mssql or all.
Using Docker Desktop with default configuration, docker can build and run both dotnet and mssql on my M4 🤔
I have a M1 + Tahoe 26.3.1 + podman 5.8.2 + AppleHV + machine-os:5.6+ this is still happening. I downgraded to machine-is:5.5 and it works fine.
